title: Tech Chat | What is NTRIP Caster?
body: Tech Chat | What is NTRIP Caster? 2023-01-10 Support Share: The NTRIP protocol is no stranger to surveyors, which consists of NTRIP Client, NTRIP Caster and NTRIP Server. During our daily surveying tasks, what we used most frequently is NTRIP Client (rover) and NTRIP Server (base). Today let’s talk about NTRIP Caster. To know about NTRIP Caster, you must know what NTRIP is first. Simple understanding, NTRIP is a protocol for differential data transmission through the network.  Read previous blog for the simple concept of NTRIP: Tech Chat - What is NTRIP? Ntrip Source is used to generate GNSS correction data and submit the data to Ntrip Server; Ntrip Server is responsible for submitting GNSS correction data to Ntrip Caster; Ntrip Caster is the correction data center, responsible for receiving and sending GNSS data; After Ntrip Client logs in, Ntrip Caster sends GNSS correction data to it. At present, most of the receivers can realize the function of Ntrip Server. The receiver sends the generated correction data to Ntrip Caster through the network, and Ntrip Caster can receive correction data from different base stations at the same time. There are two options for Ntrip Caster to send correction data to Ntrip Client: One is to directly forward the correction data generated by Ntrip Source, and Ntrip Client only needs to select a specific mount point. Ntrip Server may have multiple mount points and use different names to distinguish them. The second is to generate a virtual short-distance reference station (usually within a few meters of the rover) for the Ntrip Client by solving the correction data of multiple Ntrip sources. In this case, Ntrip Client needs to send its own coordinates to Ntrip Caster, and Ntrip Caster can thus generate a virtual reference station (VRS). For SV100 GNSS receiver, it was designed with Ntrip server and Ntrip Caster. So it can work as a single reference station and send correction data through Ntrip Caster without extra software. You need to configure both Ntrip Server and Ntrip Caster when you use this protocol. The setting is shown below:  Configuration of Ntrip Server: Set the Ntrip Server address and Nrtip Server port; this IP address is the same as SV100 IP address, and the port should be mapped by router.  Enter custom username and password  Access point: the Ntrip Caster mount point Enable Active button of Ntrip Server, Press OK to save the configuration  Configuration of Ntrip Caster:  Enter port, this port should be the same as Ntrip Server  Enter Password, this password should also be the same as Ntrip Server  Enable Active button of Ntrip Caster, press OK to save configuration  All configurations of Ntrip Caster are shown above, then you can use the rover to log in to the public network IP and port of SV100 to obtain correction data. If you don't have a static IP, you can also use DDNS to log in through the domain name and port. Read for details  Tech Chat | An optimal solution for single continuous operation base station Learn more about SV100 https://www.singularxyz.com/SV100.html

title: Tech Chat | How to make your RTK work with GIS software?
body: Tech Chat | How to make your RTK work with GIS software? 2023-01-17 Chloe Share: Although both belong to the geospatial industry, GIS and surveying have different tendencies in the selection of equipment for point surveying work. GISers are more concerned about the compatibility with GIS software and the portability of equipment, while surveyors pay more attention to the accuracy and measurement-related performance.  For GIS collection work, we normally select the portable devices, such as SingularXYZ  P1 receiver  and  T8 Pro tablet . As for land surveying, we would choose professional RTK receivers like  Y1 GNSS receiver . But sometimes surveyors may come across ad-hoc GIS collection projects. It's not worth buying an expert GIS receiver for ad-hoc tasks since they already have RTK equipment. Is it possible to use RTK receivers with GIS software to perform tasks? In this blog, we will take Y1 GNSS receiver as an example to introduce how to use RTK receiver for GIS tasks.  Part 1: Send RTK coordinates to PDA internal GPS using SingularPad software as intermediary Part 2: Developer function – Convert real-time WGS84 BLH coordinates to local coordinates Part 1 - Send RTK coordinates to PDA internal GPS using SingularPad software as intermediary 1. Connect Y1 GNSS receiver with SingularPAD  2. Setup Y1 as a Ntrip Rover For more details of work mode configuration please refer to our SingularPad tutorials on YouTube. https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiFQs75959ep_Hm4KI0UBf0RP3Zf2f6de 3. Enable Mock location service on your controller/PDA device and choose SingularPad. 4. Enable the location services on SingularPad. Then the Y1 RTK positioning results will be shared to the Android location service. You can choose any GIS software and use the Internal GPS(GNSS) mode to get the Y1 RTK results location and conduct the GIS collection work. Part 2: Developer function – Convert real-time WGS84 BLH coordinates to local coordinates Unlike surveyors who need to set the projected coordinate systems before surveying, most GIS data collection software will use the WGS84 BLH coordinates for data collection by default. So GIS users always need to convert the collection points from the WGS84 BLH coordinates to the local XYZ coordinates according to different project requirements.  In order to help simplify this process, our SingularPad location service also defined the Local coordinates interface for GIS platform/software developers. So if you are a GIS developer and need to get local coordinates, you can contact us for the development guide.

title: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 1
body: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 1 2023-01-24 Support Share: For beginners who want to build their own CORS network, the first thing they need to consider is how to build a CORS platform in order to manage all your stations and different usage rights for users. However, to purchase CORS software and get public IP or domain may be confusing for fresh beginners.  In today's blog, we will introduce you to a free trail of building your CORS platform with Amazon Cloud and SNIP. After the actual operation, I believe it will be of great reference significance for you to build a long-term platform in the future. This chapter will show you how to get a static IP with Amazon Cloud. And in the next blog we will introduce you how to create a port for data transmission, and how to use SV100 to send data to SNIP. It should be reminded here that if we experience the Amazon Cloud server account for free for the first time, Amazon Cloud supports a single cloud server for 750 hours free, and the excess part needs to be paid, so we can terminate the instance if we do not use it. Part 1: Sign in Amazon  Go to the Amazon website ( https://aws.amazon.com/?nc1=h_ls ) and first sign up for an account. Because it involves personal information, so we do not introduce how to register an Amazon account. If you already have an account, you can log in directly. Step 1: Click the upper right corner to log in Step 2: Enter the user name and password Step 3: After login, search for the EC2 module in the search bar. Part 2: Launch a new instance Step 1: Click on the upper right corner to create the instance  Step 2: Enter a name and select Windows as the Quick Start type.  Step 3: Select the instance type and select Create new key pair  Step 4: Then you need to enter the name of the new key, choose the type and finally create. Attention: After the creation is complete, a file of the key pair will be generated. Please remember its storage location and this file will be used later. Step 5: Next, create the security group Step 6: Finally, launch this instance Step 7: Select the connect to your instance after the launch succeeds.  Part 3: Gets the password for the remote connection Step 1: Select RDP client and click Get Password Step 2: Here you need to enter your private key. Step 3: Open the saved key file in TXT format and copy the document content into it. Then select Get Password. Finally, you will have the user name and password for the RDP remote connection. Part 4: Connection Step 1: Searching for MST in the search bar on your computer will bring up options for remote desktop features. Enable this function and enter the public IP address, user name and password to access the remote desktop. Step 2: The first login takes a little longer to load, and after a few minutes, you have a remote computer with a public IP address. It's running all the time. (750 hours for free for the first time) You can then install SNIP on the remote machine and get the base station data from the SV100. How to get SV100 to properly transfer data to SNIP will be covered in the next chapter. Learn more about our SV100 CORS solution https://www.singularxyz.com/SV100.html

title: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 2
body: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 2 2023-01-31 Support Share: For fresh beginners to setup their CORS network, a period free trail of maintaining your CORS stations and managing your users is important for you to build a long-term platform in the future. In the last chapter we’ve introduced how to get a static IP with Amazon Cloud.  Read previous chapter:  Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 1 Today let's learn how to use Amazon Cloud to create ports for data transfer, and how to set CORS stations and make the mountpoint online. And in the next chapter, we will introduce to you how to realize user management. Part 1: Create a transmission port in Amazon Cloud Step 1: Search for the EC2 module in the search bar and click it to enter Step 2: Select Instance in the left column, choose the instance you created before, details are displayed below. Step 3: Click Security, and next click Security groups  Step 4: Click Edit inbound rules Step 5: Click Add rule to create a port like 8888. Don't forget to save it. Then you will get a port for data transmission.  Part 2: SNIP installation and settings  Step 1: Install SNIP on your remote computer Step 2: Launch SNIP after installation, then click the IP map to modify IP and port. Here you need to enter the private IP and the previously created port. The private IP and public IP will be shown on the desktop. Step 3: Click to connect, then the status will change to listening. Step 4: Then you need to set a mountpoint for your CORS station, here we take SV100 as an example. Go to Pushed-in stream and click Add. Click Configure. Set name, user and password for it Part 3: CORS station settings – SV100 GNSS receiver Step 1: Log in the webpage of SV100, go to Work Management >> Data Transmission >> NTRIP Server, click Config. Step 2: Enter all the parameters same as SNIP settings, including the Caster Address (public IP), Port, User, Password and Mount Point. Then select the data type you need and tick Startup. Finally click OK to save all the settings. Step 3: Finally you will find the mountpoint singularxyz is online. Note: If you have not registered with SNIP, it is only online for 1 hour, and you can experience all functions within an hour and manage 5 CORS stations at the same time. After an hour you can restart SNIP to get back online. If you register SNIP for free, you can permanently use the basic functions and keep 3 CORS stations online for a long time. More about the SV100 CORS solution, https://www.singularxyz.com/SV100.html

title: Tech Chat | A Convenient Function for GIS – SingularPad Zigzag
body: Tech Chat | A Convenient Function for GIS – SingularPad Zigzag 2023-02-07 Support Share: Geographic location attributes are the basic attributes of all things in the real world, so geographic information services (GIS) are important applications closely related to our lives, such as urban planning, natural resource management, urban pipeline management, architectural design, logistics, autonomous driving and other maps data-related scenarios. With the development of the GNSS industry, RTK has also become the basic tool for GIS data collection. Our RTK field software is also designed with functions such as coordinate collection, coding, automatic mapping, and result export. In this blog, we will introduce ZigZag, a useful function for feature collection. It can automatically switch feature codes during work and allow surveyors to focus more on the environment, which is often used for linear acquisition tasks. Let us follow the example below to understand its application scenarios and usage methods. Working scenarios For example, when working near the road as in the figure shown below, GISers normally need to collect feature points on the road for drawing, such as footpath, kerb, road centerlines and so on. For such a long line measurement task, surveyors often need to measure these features alternately. With the Zigzag function, the feature code will be automatically switched, so that surveyors can keep an eye on passing cars, rather than paying attention to the software to switch codes, which can protect their own safety and work efficiently. So how are we going to collect this road？ Firstly, we need to set an interval, such as collecting every 10 meters, starting from one side of the road and collecting feature points on the road in turn. It's on the other side of the road when it's done. When this row is finished, walk 10 meters, then collect the next row, and then start on the other side. Then collect in turn. The sequence is left → right → 10 meters forward → right → left → 10 meters forward → left → right... , as shown below: How to conduct Zigzag with SingularPad in such a situation? First, SingularPad is used to connect the receiver via Bluetooth, and make it fixed. Then go to the Point Survey, open Settings, and enable zigzag at the bottom. Then click the list of Code to add codes. Note: Kerb, footpath, and center line of the road are all linear elements. So you need to select the type as a polyline at creation time, and then create the three codes. Then go back to the survey interface, select the one of these codes, then set the line name, and measurement. For example, the first code is footpath and the line name is set to 1. Then click the Measure button to collect the first point P1, which is the starting point of the first footpath. Then select kerb, set the line name to 1, and click the Measure button to capture the first point P2, which is the starting point of the first kerb. Then select center line, set the line name to 1, and click the Measure button to capture the first point, P3, which is the starting point of the first center line. Then select kerb, set the line name to 2, and click the Measure button to capture the first point P4, which is the starting point of the second kerb. Finally, select footpath, set the line name to 2, and click the Measure button to capture the first point P5, which is the starting point of the second footpath. After the first round of collection is completed, the software interface

title: Tech Chat | How accurate can GNSS achieve without base stations?
body: Tech Chat | How accurate can GNSS achieve without base stations? 2023-02-14 Chloe Share: In most applications of GNSS positioning, we usually use RTK technology to obtain centimeter-level precise positioning. The principle of RTK is to use the correction data from base stations or CORS to correct the positioning data for higher accuracy. But without these base stations, do you know how accurate GNSS can be? In today's blog, we will introduce the original accuracy of GNSS single-point mode and two optimized methods to obtain higher accuracy without base stations. 1. Single Point Mode The single-point mode is the positioning accuracy obtained by GNSS without any correction and optimization, which can reach meter-level accuracy. GNSS modules/receivers with better performance on the market can generally achieve an accuracy of about 2-3m. In our daily life, the built-in GPS chip of our mobile phone adopts the single-point positioning mode, which can meet the needs of daily map navigation. 2. SBAS Mode The principle of SBAS mode is quite like RTK, it receives GNSS and SBAS signals at the same time, eliminating regional error to obtain higher accuracy. Learn more about SBAS:  Tech Chat | What is SBAS? Different from the RTK base station that particularly corrects rover in certain range (>20km), the SBAS mode is a regional correction that can cover multiple countries or a continent. Therefore, the improvement of SBAS accuracy cannot reach the level of RTK. Normally the accuracy of SBAS mode can reach <1m 3D RMS, which can be used for some GIS projects or agricultural navigation systems that do not strictly require high accuracy. Learn more about SingularXYZ products that support SBAS mode. https://www.singularxyz.com/Y1.html https://www.singularxyz.com/SV100.html https://www.singularxyz.com/SAgro100_Automated_Steering_System.html 3. Doppler Smooth Mode Except using correction data to improve the accuracy, there is also other way to use Doppler algorithms to improve the positioning accuracy. During the movement of the terminal, the Doppler algorithm can smooth the GNSS positioning point according to the movement trajectory of the terminal, so as to achieve higher dynamic positioning accuracy. The accuracy of Doppler single-point smooth Mode can reach 15-30cm, which is suitable for some vehicle mounted systems that need dynamic positioning data, such as agricultural guidance systems. Learn more about SingularXYZ products that support Doppler smooth mode, https://www.singularxyz.com/SAgro10_GNSS_Guidance_System.html

title: Tech Chat | How to simplify your total station stakeout?
body: Tech Chat | How to simplify your total station stakeout? 2023-02-21 Chloe Share: How are your stakeout tasks with traditional total stations? Repeatedly observe and then instruct the prism operator to the right position? Or even use the intercom for communication? Today we will introduce you a new function to largely simplify your stakeout tasks! Considering the complex process of traditional total station stakeout, SingularXYZ team has added the Stakeout Position Sharing function in SingularPad field software. Total station observer can share the real-time stakeout position to the prism operator through SingularPad, then realize easy stake out according to the software prompts. Watch the whole process on our YouTube channel https://youtu.be/nfRL34KksK0 Step 1: Connect the total station to SingularPad and set station Turn on the Bluetooth of TS1000 total station, select the device type of SingularPad as total station and then connect. After connection, you can see the real-time angle parameters (VA & HA) are shown on the top status bar of SingularPad. Then you can set station in the software. Method 1: Set station & orientation Method 2: Free set station Step 2: Sharing stakeout information Go to Survey >> Point Stakeout >> To-Stake-Out, manually add or import the points that you need to stakeout, then go to the stakeout interface. Click the sharing button, SingularPad will share the stakeout information via the QR code. Step 3: Get the stakeout prompt with another phone Use the phone of the prism operator to scan the QR code, you can get the real-time stakeout prompt via SingularPad.  Now you can simply follow the software prompt to finish the stakeout tasks. Learn more about our TS1000 total station and field software SingularPad https://www.singularxyz.com/TS1000.html https://www.singularxyz.com/SingularPad.html

title: Tech Chat | How to build a CORS station? – Chapter 1
body: Tech Chat | How to build a CORS station? – Chapter 1 2023-02-28 Support Share: With the development of high precision GNSS industry and RTK positioning technology, more and more countries and areas started to build their local CORS. Even some terminal users of surveying, precision agriculture and smart navigation start to build personal CORS for their own use. As introduced in previous blogs, we've already know the increasing importance of CORS. Today, let's learn together how to build a CORS station. The first step of building a CORS station is to choose a good location, which will depend on several factors. Read the previous blog:  Tech Chat | How To Site A CORS Station? After site the location, we need to collect GNSS signal data and check if the data quality is sufficient to build a CORS station. In this chapter, we will take SV100 GNSS receiver as an example to see how to check the GNSS data quality. Preparation CORS GNSS receiver *1 ( SV100 GNSS receiver  as an example) GNSS antenna *1 ( SA500 choke ring antenna  as an example) GNSS antenna cable *1 Tripod *1 Tribrach *1 Post-processing software *1 (PProcessing software as an example) Step 1: Record 24h GNSS static data 1. Set up tripod and tribrach, then install SA500 antenna on the tripod. 2. Power on SV100 and connect SA500 to SV100 with the antenna cable. Login the web page of SV100 via Ethernet cable or Wi-Fi and check whether the satellites tracking status is normal. 3. Go to Work Management >> Data Recording, select one record and click Config. Then choose to record 24H data as shown in the image below and click to Start Recording. Note : Please keep the power supply stable in the following 24 hours. 4. After 24 hours, you can download the data record via SV100 web page, then import it into PProcessing software. Step 2: Analyze the data quality 1. Right click the data file in PProcessing software and click File Analyse. For professional CORS construction project, you can enter the Warning parameters in the image according to the project requirements. As for personal use, you can directly keep the default settings. After settings, click Start to analyze the GNSS data quality. If all indicators pass, it means that the location is ideal for building a CORS station. Now you can start building your CORS station there, and in the next chapter we'll show you how to build it. Learn more about our CORS solution and

title: Tech Chat | How to build a CORS station? – Chapter 2
body: Tech Chat | How to build a CORS station? – Chapter 2 2023-03-07 Support Share: In the previous chapters, we talked about how to site an ideal location for a CORS station and analyze GNSS data quality. After these preparations, we can start building the CORS station. The construction of the CORS station includes two parts, outdoor and indoor, as shown in the figure below. The outdoor part includes a GNSS antenna, a lightning rod and solar panels (optional). The indoor part includes a GNSS receiver like the SV100, antenna surge protectors, power and internet. For the outdoor construction part, how to set up the GNSS antenna should be the top priority. In today's blog, we will take the SA500 choke ring antenna as an example to show you how to choose a suitable GNSS antenna installation method. Based on different installation environments, there are usually three ways to install the antenna. Method 1: Side Wall Installation environment requirements This method is built on the roof parapet. The storey height does not exceed 30 meters, the building age does not exceed 20 years, and the building is a multi-storey concrete structure. The installation environment requires a solid wall with a thickness greater than 20CM and a height greater than 70CM. The site should comprehensively consider the lightning protection environment, and the wall should be grounded. If the lightning protection coverage is not enough, it is necessary to add lightning rods. Installation procedure Step 1: First, you need to drill holes in the wall and clean the holes. The first choice for fixing bolts is chemical bolts. Step 2: There must be fixing screws in the four screw holes of each support rod seat, and there must be no omissions. The distance between the bases of the two support rods should be 30cm-60cm, which can be adjusted according to the height of the wall. In order to ensure stability and safety, the height of the steel pipe protruding from the wall should be 30cm. Step 3: A weight plate should be installed on top of the steel pipe. The side wall pipe should be perpendicular to the horizontal plane during installation. The antenna is generally higher than the wall. Step 4: Install the GNSS antenna. The antenna cable from the antenna to the internal equipment room must be protected by a protective tube. The size of the protection tube must be appropriate (refer to a water pipe with a diameter of 30mm), and all connections must be sealed. Step 5: Install the lightning rod, make sure that there is an insulator between the lightning rod and the side wall rod, and they cannot be in direct contact. And solder the lightning rod lead to the ground.  Method 2: Steel Pier Installation environment requirements When there is no qualified structural roof to install the side wall scheme, the steel pier scheme is adopted. The site has a wide field of view, and the field of view should not be blocked by obstacles with an elevation angle greater than 10°. There should be no multipath effects around the site, that is, objects that strongly reflect satellite signals, such as large glass walls and large metal billboards. The load-bearing capacity of the roof meets the overall weight requirements. The site should comprehensively consider the lightning protection environment, and the wall should be grounded. If the lightning protection coverage is not enough, it is necessary to add lightning rods. Installation procedure Step 1: First, set up the base and fill it with ceme

title: Tech Chat | How to combine total station & RTK receiver surveying?
body: Tech Chat | How to combine total station & RTK receiver surveying? 2023-03-14 Chloe Share: To obtain higher efficiency, nowadays more and more large-scale projects combine total station and GNSS receivers to complete complex survey tasks. But in this case, the most troublesome thing is the subsequent processing of point data in different coordinate systems. If data problems are found after processing, users may even need to do some re-measurement. In this blog, we will introduce how to perform both total station and RTK receiver surveys in the same coordinate system through SingularPad software. Post-processing is no longer required, users can view the surveying status in real time and check for problems in time. Preparation: At least 2 known points of the local coordinate system. Total station:  TS1000 total station . GNSS RTK receiver:  Y1 GNSS receiver . SingularPad  field software. Step 1: Set station for total station Create a new project file in SingularPad and keep the default coordinate system parameters. Turn on the Bluetooth of TS1000 total station, select the Device Type of SingularPad as total station and then connect. Then you can set station based on the known points. Step 2: Set coordinate system for GNSS receiver Switch the Device Type as GNSS and connect the Y1 GNSS receiver to SingularPad. Go to Project >> Localization, use the 3 known points coordinates to transform the geodetic coordinate system to your local system. Watch the detailed tutorial:  SingularPad Tutorial | Localization Step 3: Start surveying tasks You can fluently switch between total stations and GNSS receiver for surveying. The surveying points of both will be in the same local coordinate system, users can easily export the data in various formats, such as *.csv, *.dxf, *.txt, etc. Learn more about our TS1000 total station and field software SingularPad https://www.singularxyz.com/TS1000.html https://www.singularxyz.com/SingularPad.html

title: Tech Chat | How to make your RTK work with Mobile Topographer?
body: Tech Chat | How to make your RTK work with Mobile Topographer? 2023-03-23 Support Share: Different users have their own work habits and preferred software. In order to facilitate our vast user groups, our RTK products are compatible with common field surveying software and GIS software on the market. Today we will take Mobile Topographer as an example to see how we can make our Y1 GNSS receiver work with it. Preparation: RTK device, such as Y1 GNSS receiver, P1 GNSS receiver, T8 Pro GNSS tablet, etc. Your preferred software, such as Mobile Topographer SingularSurv or SingularPad software To make SingularXYZ RTK devices work with other software, you only need to enable a small function in Singularpad or SingularSurv. In a previous blog, we've already introduced the steps of SingularPad, today let’s focus on SinguarSurv. Read previous blog:  Tech Chat | How to make your RTK work with GIS software? Step 1: Connect Y1 GNSS receiver with SingularSurv and get fixed solution Open SingularSurv, and connect with Y1. Click Rover and create a new work mode, making it fixed. Here use PDA CORS as an example. Step 2: Turn on mock location function Go to setting, and enable Mock Location, which can share the coordinate information to your PDA device. Then other software can directly use the positioning data, like Mobile Topographer. Step 3: Select SingularSurv as mock location app in your PDA device If you are using SC100 data collector or T8 tablet, you need to take an extra step. Go to Setting >> System >> About phone >> Build number, quickly click it five times to enable developer options. Go to developer options in your PDA system settings, and then go to select mock location app. Click and choose SingularSurv. Step 4: Start your tasks Open Mobile Topographer or other software, it will get coordinate information from your RTK device now.

title: Tech Chat – What’s the difference between RTK & RTD?
body: Tech Chat – What’s the difference between RTK & RTD? 2023-03-28 Support Share: In order to obtain higher accuracy of GNSS, we usually use DGPS (Differential Global Positioning System) to help improve accuracy. Both using the differential data to correct GNSS positioning, what’s the main difference between RTK (Real Time Kinematic) and RTD (Real Time Differential)? And what are the differences in their application fields? Let's discuss it in today's blog. RTD (Real Time Differential) As a type of DGPS technology, RTD also consists of ground base stations and rovers. The rover receives the correction data sent by the base station to improve the positioning accuracy. The main difference is in the content of the correction data. RTD calculates the true distance based on the known coordinates of the reference station and the coordinates of each satellite, and calculates the pseudo-range from each satellite to the reference station at each moment. Then compare it with the measured pseudo-range, deduce the pseudo-range correction number and send it to the user receiver, which is a code (C/A, P) difference technology. Since RTD uses pseudo-range code difference, which does not have high requirements for ionospheric errors, the base station range is up to 100km, and it's easy to fix. The accuracy of RTD positioning is typically sub-meter level. Pseudo-range code C/A P Length period of pseudo-noise code 1023bit 1023bit Code element width about 293.1m 29.3m Repetition period of pseudo-noise code 1ms 0.097752μs Clock pulse rate 1.023Mbit/s 10.23 Mb/s RTK (Real Time Kinematic) For RTK, although the working principle is roughly the same as that of RTD, the content of its correction data is more advanced. RTK calculates the carrier phase observation value of two stations, that is, sends the carrier phase collected by the base station to the user receiver, and solves the coordinates through difference data, which belongs to the carrier (L1, L2, L5) phase difference technology. As shown in the figure below, since the code rate of the carrier is higher than the pseudo-range, the positioning accuracy of RTK is also higher, usually up to centimeter level. But on the other hand, RTK is more difficult to fix than RTD, and its cost is also higher. (a) Code (P), (b) Code (C/A) (c) Carrier phase Fig. 2 Waveforms of different codes   Conclusion  In summary, both RTD and RTK belong to DGPS. The accuracy of RTD can reach the sub-meter level, and it is more cost-effective, which can be used in projects with moderate accuracy requirements such as vehicle navigation and agricultural spraying. But if you need higher positioning accuracy, RTK will be a better choice for land surveying, machine guidance, etc. Method RTD RTK DGPS √ √ Differential Technology Code (C/A, P) Carrier phase (L1, L2, L5) Accuracy Meter level or sub-meter level Centimeter level Working Range >100km >20km Fixed Difficulty Easy Moderate Nowadays, with the rapid development of the GNSS industry, the RTK technology is more advanced than before. With full constellation tracking, 2.5cm accuracy, high fixed rate even in harsh environments, SingularXYZ team keeps making RTK products and solutions more cost-effective and easy-to-use.

title: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS server for free with Alibaba Cloud?
body: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS server for free with Alibaba Cloud? 2023-04-04 Support Share: In our previous blogs, we introduced a free method using Amazon Cloud & SNIP for beginners who want to build their own CORS platform. However, some of our SV100 users recently said that the 750hrs free trial period of Amazon Cloud is a bit short. So in today's blog, we will share a new method of free trial throughout the year – how to build your CORS server with Alibaba Cloud? Read previous blogs: Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 1 Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 2 Part 1: Create your account 1. Go to the official website of Alibaba Cloud ( https://alibabacloud.com ), log in or register a new account. 2. Go to Products >> Featured Product, select Elastic Compute Service (ECS). There are many free trials you can try. 3. In the package settings, you can leave most of the settings as default for free trial. But please follow the guideline of this blog for the following settings. Choose Windows Server as shown in the picture. In the Networking setting, you should check the Assign Public IPV4 Address option so that it can get a public address. Then set the login username and password in System Configurations. You can remote connect this server using the IP address and password. 4. When all the settings are done, you can preview them before submitting, and the total amount should be 0.00 USD. The server will be created within 1-5 minutes of submission. Part 2: Server configuration 1. You can go to the Menu to check the details of the instance again.   2. Map a port for CORS software.  Go to Network & Security >> Security Groups, you need to add a rule in the default security group. Click Add Rules >> Add Rule. Select the port number you need in Dest option, here we use 9999 as an example. Then set other options as in the picture below and click save. Part 3: Install CORS software on your server 1. Searching for MST in the search bar on your computer will bring up options for remote desktop features. Enable this function and enter the public IP address, user name and password to access the remote desktop. 2. You can now install CORS software on the server. Read the previous blog for the software part,  Tech Chat | How to build your CORS platform for free? – Chapter 2

title: Tech Chat | Should I Choose A Choke Ring Antenna For My Reference Station?
body: Tech Chat | Should I Choose A Choke Ring Antenna For My Reference Station? 2023-04-11 Chloe Share: Since our SV100 GNSS receiver was released half a year ago, many users have shown great interest and got positive feedback about it. Most of our users have used the SV100 as a reference station for their CORS or monitoring projects. When supporting our SV100 users building reference stations, one of the most frequently asked questions is - should I use a choke ring antenna for SV100? What are the advantages of choke ring antenna over geodetic antenna? In today's blog, we will give a brief introduction of Choke Ring antenna and hope this will help you to make a decision. What Is Choke Ring Antenna? The structural design of the choke ring antenna has been proven worldwide to effectively reduce the strength of most multipath signals. It consists of a circular ring with a number of slots or gaps in it, which effectively act as wave guides to cancel out the reflected signals. The ring is typically made of a conductive material, such as metal, and is placed above a ground plane to further reduce interference. A choke ring antenna is a type of GNSS antenna that is designed to mitigate the effects of multipath interference. It is usually a quarter wavelength deep, in order to create a high impedance surface that prevents propagation of surface waves near the antenna and excitation of undesired modes. The net effect is a very smooth controlled pattern with low susceptibility to multipath. What Is Multipath Interference? Multipath interference occurs when the satellite signal is reflected off a surface, such as a building or a vehicle, and arrives at the antenna at different times, causing errors in the GNSS measurements.  When GNSS signals reach the choke ring antenna, they pass through the gaps between the rings and are received by the antenna element. The reflected signals, on the other hand, are blocked by the rings and are not received by the antenna element. This helps to ensure that the signals received by the antenna are only the direct signals from the GNSS satellites, which results in more accurate positioning. Therefore, choke ring antennas are commonly used in high-precision GNSS applications to reduce the multipath error, such as national CORS and large monitoring projects etc. Is the Choke Ring Antenna Essential? In addition to the choke antenna, there is also a small type of GNSS antenna that can be used in reference stations – the geodetic antenna. Judging from the market feedback, it also has a good performance and is worth recommending. Compared with geodetic antennas, choke ring antennas are obviously more professional. But correspondingly, the price of the choke ring antenna is also quite high, generally four or five times higher than that of the geodetic antenna. In conclusion, for users who have sufficient budget and plan to build a professional CORS network, the choke ring antenna is the first choice. But if you just build a station for your own use without strict project requirements, geodetic antennas will be your cost-effective solution. SingularXYZ SA100 Geodetic Antenna and SA500 Choke Ring Antenna are ready for your difference demands. https://www.singularxyz.com/SA500.html https://www.singularxyz.com/SA100.html

title: Tech Chat | How to measure AB guidance line for auto-steering systems?
body: Tech Chat | How to measure AB guidance line for auto-steering systems? 2023-04-18 Chloe Share: When using an auto-steering system for farming tasks, the first thing is to create the route. In common Straight line mode, normally we will drive the tractor to the start of the line to mark point A, and drive to the end of the line to mark point B. Then the software will generate the auto-steering route based on these two points. However, in some large farms, it is a big waste of fuel to drive across the farm just to generate the route. That’s why we need a portable GNSS terminal to measure the AB line and import to the auto-steering system. Meanwhile, a portable GNSS receiver can also help farmers to measure the boundary of farms and plots for smart management. In this blog, we will introduce how to use a portable GNSS receiver to help generate the auto-steering route. Preparation: SAgro100 automated steering system. A portable GNSS terminal, such as P1 GNSS receiver, T8 Pro tablet, Y1 GNSS receiver, etc. SingularPad field surveying software. Step 1: Measure AB line Create a new project and set the Ellipsoid Parameter as WGS-84. For other settings, you can directly keep the default value. Connect your portable terminal to SingularPad software and get a fixed RTK solution. For the detailed operation, please refer to the YouTube tutorial videos: SingularPad Tutorial | Device Connection      SingularPad Tutorial | PDA CORS Work Mode Click Tools >> AB Line, move to the start of the guidance line to click to measure point A, then go to measure the point B. Step 2: Export AB line Click Export to export the AB line file. If you are using SAgro100 automated steering system, please select the file type as *.ini. If you are using other auto-steering systems, you can select the *.shp file format. Step 3: Import AB line Copy the AB line file into an USB and insert the USB into T10 tablet. Open SAgro100 system software, click AB >> Manage >> USB to import an existing AB line via USB. And you can start auto-steering tasks now. In addition to the above method, SingularSurv software of SingularXYZ can also be used to measure the AB guidance line. Contact our support team (support@singularxyz.com) to get the FAQ document. Learn more about SingularXYZ AB Guidance Line Surveying Solution.

title: Tech Chat | How do solar storms affect RTK surveying?
body: Tech Chat | How do solar storms affect RTK surveying? 2023-04-25 Support Share: For sophisticated surveyors, you may have experienced a time when RTK equipment suddenly became difficult to fix, even with perfect equipment in an open sky view. After that everything went back to normal. This is most likely caused by a solar storm. Recently, you may also feel that RTK devices have become difficult to fix around the world, such as Vietnam and India. Because it is currently the active period of solar storms. So how do solar storms affect RTK surveying? What's the impact of 2023 solar storms? In such a special period, what should we do to make the measurement results reliable? What is the Ionosphere Error? In RTK measurement, the satellite signal will pass through the atmosphere and be received by the receiver, and various errors will occur in the process. Among them, due to the active ionospheric high-energy particles, it will have a double impact on satellite signals, which can not only change the propagation time of the signal, but also affect the propagation path of the signal. The ionosphere is constantly changing. The ionosphere consists of particles that are ionized by solar radiation, so the ionosphere changes with sunrise and sunset. At night, when it is not exposed to the sun, the previously ionized particles recombine to become neutral particles and the ionosphere becomes less concentrated. In addition to solar radiation, the ionosphere is subject to many unpredictable changes from various sources of perturbation in space and on Earth itself, which makes it impossible to accurately determine the ionospheric pattern at a particular time. In RTK algorithms, ionospheric delay can be eliminated by double difference, which is based on the assumption that the delay from the satellite transmitting end to the ground base station receiving end and the satellite transmitting end to the ground mobile station receiving end are consistent, and the consistency between them will become worse as the baseline grows and the ionospheric activity increases. In the long baseline RTK calculation, the currently popular linear combination of ionosphere-free can eliminate the influence of the ionospheric first-order term, but at the same time amplify the noise and multi-path effect; in the virtual reference station algorithm of network RTK, there are also studies of interpolation model algorithms based on baseline length and ionospheric activity, but when the ionosphere is overly active, the accuracy of the virtual reference station data will The accuracy of VRS data is greatly reduced when the ionosphere is overactive. How Do Solar Storms Affect RTK? As mentioned earlier, the ionosphere consists of particles that are ionized by solar radiation. Therefore, during the active period of the solar storm, the ionosphere will be highly active due to its influence, which will increase the RTK measurement error, make it difficult to fix, and reduce the accuracy. Impact of Solar Storms on GNSS in 2023 When judging the impact of solar storms, we usually use the index F10.7, which is the solar 10.7cm radio flux (10-22w.m-2.Hz-1), which is used to represent the heating and ionization effect. The Space Environment Prediction Center regularly releases F10.7 index short- and medium-term forecast products and evaluates and validates the F10.7 index short-term forecast results. The forecast quantity evaluated is the F10.7 forecast value for the next three days, and the

title: Tech Chat | How to make your surveying more accurate? – Importance of Grid Shift
body: Tech Chat | How to make your surveying more accurate? – Importance of Grid Shift 2023-05-02 Support Share: In daily surveying, we usually set up base stations on known points. What if there are no known points or the known points are far apart in some large-scale projects? How can we ensure the accuracy when shift or re-start the base station? Relative accuracy & Absolute accuracy In this situation, first we need to clarify the definition of Relative accuracy and Absolute accuracy. Absolute accuracy is the comparison between the measured points and the known points, while relative accuracy is the comparison between multiple measurement points. So under this no known points project, what we should guarantee is the relative accuracy results. But caused by the single solution on GNSS Receiver is meters accuracy, each time when setup base, the base value also has the meters shift. Then the RTK rovers results will also have much difference if compared even at the same point. Therefore, in projects without known points, what we need to ensure is the relative accuracy of the survey results. But since the single-point solution of most RTK receivers is meter-level accuracy, every time you set up the base station with auto-base mode on the same point, the base positioning may have a meter-level offset. Then the results for RTK rovers can vary greatly even when compared at the same point. How Grid Shift helps in this situation? In view of this situation, the method Grid Shift was born. Assuming that you are starting a survey at the site for the first time, you can start the base station in auto-base mode, and then conduct the survey tasks normally. At the next day when the base station needs to be started again, you can directly select the previously measured point as the base station point to start the base station, so as to ensure the relative accuracy of the two operations. In the follow-up tasks of this project, you can continue to use this method to start the base station to ensure relative accuracy. How to use Grid Shift function:  SingularPad Tutorial | Calibrate Point If the local known points coordinates are obtained later in the project, you can also use the localization function to shift all the measured points of the current project to the known point coordinate system. How to use Localization function:  SingularPad Tutorial | Localization SingularXYZ SingularPad field surveying software can help you carry out measurement projects smoothly, learn more: https://www.singularxyz.com/SingularPad.html

title: Tech Chat | How to test the accuracy of IMU?
body: Tech Chat | How to test the accuracy of IMU? 2023-05-09 Support Share: In past blogs, we've introduced what IMU is and how it works on our Y1 GNSS receiver. Nowadays, more and more GNSS receivers integrate IMU module for greater convenience and higher efficiency. So to judge the performance of the IMU function become a focus. Today, we will introduce a conventional method of testing the accuracy of IMU, so that you can test your own equipment. Preparation: GNSS receiver, here we will take Y1 as an example. Field surveying software, in this blog we will use SingularPad. PDA device. Test Scene: Here we will introduce several typical scenes, you can select one fixed point in each scene. If you have enough time or want to test in more detail, you can also set more points in one environment. Scene 1: Open sky Scene 2: Under the tree Scene 3: Near the building IMU Test: Before the test, you should make sure your GNSS receiver gets a fixed RTK solution and initialize the IMU function. Related tutorials:  SingularSurv Software Tutorial Video Y1 Tutorial | Y1 GNSS Receiver IMU Calibration Step 1: Turn off the IMU function and measure each test point while centering the pole. The point coordinates will be used as a reference. Step 2: Turn on the IMU function, measure each point at different tilt angles and tilt directions. Tilt direction: East, west, north and south. Tilt direction: Y1 GNSS receiver support up to 60° tilt surveying, so we can choose several angles between 0 - 60° at an interval of 10°, which should be 0°, 10°, 20°, 30°, 40°, 50° and 60°. Then we will get 16 sets of point coordinates for each test point. Step 3: Compare the point coordinates of each test point with its reference, the error should be the accuracy of the IMU function. You can now see how the IMU accuracy varies with the tilt angle increasing and how horizontal accuracy and vertical accuracy clearly. As shown in the picture above, the IMU error of our Y1 GNSS receiver is less than 3cm with up to 60° tilt surveying.

title: Tech Chat | What are the sources of RTK errors and how to avoid them?
body: Tech Chat | What are the sources of RTK errors and how to avoid them? 2023-05-16 Support Share: For the application of GNSS positioning and navigation technology, such as surveying, machine control, precision agriculture, etc., RTK technology is most commonly used. So what are the main sources of RTK error? How should we avoid these errors in daily use so as to obtain more accurate positioning? Ionospheric Error When the GNSS signal passes through the ionosphere, the path of the signal will bend, and the propagation speed will also change. At this time, the distance obtained by multiplying the propagation time of the signal by the speed of light in vacuum is not equal to the geometric distance between the satellite and the receiver. This deviation is called ionospheric refraction error. Solution: RTK technology uses differential data sent from the base station to substantially correct system errors such as ionospheric errors for rovers. For this kind of RTK error, we can select a powerful GNSS RTK receiver to guarantee the RTK accuracy. Baseline Distance Error Most high-precision RTK receivers claim an accuracy of 8mm+1ppm. The ppm here means that the distance between the rover and the base increases by 1km, and the RTK error will increase by 1mm. So this is the error caused by the increase of the baseline distance. Solution: This error is an objective error of the RTK method. In daily work, it’s better to keep the distance between the rover and the base station within 20km to ensure the surveying accuracy. Multipath Error Multipath interference occurs when the satellite signal is reflected off a surface, such as a building or a vehicle, and arrives at the RTK receiver at different times, causing errors in the GNSS measurements. Solution: During the measurement, try to avoid large reflective surfaces, such as water surfaces, glass buildings, etc. For some monitoring or CORS projects, users can also use choke ring antenna to eliminate multipath errors. Satellite Signal Quality Error Poor satellite signal quality will also lead to large RTK errors. This is usually caused by severe occlusion or signal interference in the environment, such as dense forests, buildings, electric towers, etc., Solution: For the base station, it's best to set up under an open sky view. For rover, surveyors can select full-constellation RTK receivers, which can track more satellites even in harsh environments. Human Error In daily surveying, errors may also occur due to improper human operation, such as the point is not aimed, the pole is not centered, and so on. Solution: For human error, it can only be avoided by the care and experience of the surveyor. However, surveyors can also choose an RTK receiver with an IMU, which can measure without centering the pole, reducing fatigue and errors.   With the rapid development of the GNSS industry today, the powerful performance of the equipment has greatly eliminated various common errors. Therefore, choosing an advanced and reliable RTK device is the best way to get accurate results.

title: Tech Chat | What is VRS and its advantages?
body: Tech Chat | What is VRS and its advantages? 2023-05-23 Chloe Share: With the popularization of RTK technology in all walks of life, the global CORS coverage is getting higher and higher. Many surveyors, GISer, farmers, etc. started to use CORS instead of setting up RTK base stations themselves. When selecting a mount point in CORS, in addition to normal mount points, you may often see VRS, nearest, etc. What do they mean and what’s the advantage of them? Difference with normal mountpoint Compared with normal mount points, the data transmission between CORS and rover in Nearest and VRS methods is bidirectional. After selecting a normal mount point, it will transmit differential data to Rover in one way. While Nearest and VRS require Rover to upload its location information to CORS first, and CORS will then transmit the corresponding differential data to rover according to its location. Nearest The Nearest method will automatically select the differential data sent by the base station closest to the Rover according to the location of the Rover. This method allows users to directly select the nearest base station without comparing different mount points, which is the most commonly used method in CORS. VRS (Virtual Reference Station) Different from normal base stations, VRS (Virtual Reference Station) technology will generate a virtual base station beside the rover within several meters, which can largely shorten the baseline distance and improve RTK accuracy. To realize VRS technology, the CORS provider needs to set up at least 3 base stations in a certain area. Based on the coordinates and real-time observation data of each base station, the CORS software platform will solve the real-time error model of the area. And then "virtualize" a base station located around the rover according to relevant mathematical model algorithm and the obtained rover coordinates. In the past, the cost of building & maintaining a CORS reference station was pretty high, so the distribution of CORS stations was relatively sparse. At that time, VRS technology was very important to reduce the RTK error caused by long baseline distance.  However, with the development of high precision GNSS industry, the cost of CORS station construction is gradually reduced, and the distribution of CORS is gradually dense. Since VRS has higher algorithm requirements for the CORS platform software, nearest has become more commonly used.

title: Tech Chat | How to test the RTK performance?
body: Tech Chat | How to test the RTK performance? 2023-05-30 Support Share: When using RTK equipment for surveying, the most important performance of RTK should be its accuracy and re-initialization time. The former determines the work quality, while the latter determines work efficiency. Our previous blog has introduced multiple sources of RTK errors, we can tell that the RTK performance is not the same in different countries and different working environments. If you want to know exactly how your RTK receiver performs, you can test it by yourself. In today's blog, we'll introduce a simple way to test RTK performance in two aspects - accuracy and re-initialization time. Preparation: GNSS receiver, here we will take Y1 as an example. Field surveying software, in this blog we will use SingularPad. PDA device. Test Scene: Here we will introduce several typical scenes, you can select several points in each scene. If you have enough time or want to test in more detail, you can also choose more test scenes. Scene 1: Open sky Scene 2: Under the tree Scene 3: Near the building Base Station Setup: As introduced in previous blog, the shorter baseline between the higher accuracy and faster re-initialization time. In order to see the affect of the baseline distance, we need to set a long baseline and a short baseline to test separately. Short baseline: 100m between base and rover. Long baseline: 10km between base and rover. Note: You can set different baseline distances according to your daily work scenarios. Test Method: Step 1: Get reference coordinates of each test point Set the smooth GPS point as 5-10, the point coordinates of this long-time smooth RTK solution will be used as a reference. Step 2: Record the re-initialization time and measure the point coordinates after each re-initialization. At each point, invert the RTK receiver and wait for the fixed RTK solution to become single-point solution. Then put the receiver upright, record the time from single-point to the fixed solution of the receiver, and then measure the point. Repeat this process at least 5 times for each point in different test scenes. Step 3: Summarize test data. RTK accuracy: Compare the point coordinates of each test point with its reference, the error should be the accuracy of the IMU function. RTK re-initialization time: Calculate the average value of re-initialization time, you can get a typical value of your RTK device performance in different working environments. Here is a simple example of the test results of Y1 GNSS receiver. As shown in the table below, with strong satellite tracking and anti-interference capability, different environments and baseline distances have little effect on the re-initialization performance of Y1. The average time is within 3 seconds.

title: Tech Chat | How can SAgro100 be compatible with most tractors? – Importance of Spline Sleeves
body: Tech Chat | How can SAgro100 be compatible with most tractors? – Importance of Spline Sleeves 2023-06-06 Kim Share: How is the SAgro100 autosteer system compatible with most tractor models? This is the point that most users will care about. The key to this is the spline sleeves with different heights, number of spline teeth and diameters, which is essential to make sure your autosteer kit works properly. In today's blog, we will introduce how spline sleeves work and how to select a suitable spline sleeve. How do spline sleeves work? First of all, we need to know what the spline is for. Splines are parts that transmit mechanical torque. Every tractor has a steering shaft to control the direction of motion. The surface of the steering shaft usually has longitudinal keyway, and the spline sleeve has corresponding keyway. When the two keyways fit together, the spline can drive the steering shaft to keep rotating synchronously.     In the SAgro100 system, the spline is installed between the electric motor and the steering shaft of the tractor. When the motor rotates, it will drive the spline to rotate, thereby driving the steering shaft of the tractor to rotate, so as to control the steering of the tractor. How to select a suitable spline sleeve? Knowing what a spline does, we can see that the key to choosing the right spline is getting the spline to fit your tractor steering shaft. Count how many grooves your tractor steering shaft has and measure its diameter, then select a spline with the appropriate number of teeth and diameter. What does SingularXYZ provide? To fit the mainstream tractor models in the market, SingularXYZ team provides commonly used splines as follows for you to choose. If your vehicle is not included in the table below, we can also customize a new spline based on the number of teeth and diameter of your tractor steering shaft.

title: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 1 Signal Connection
body: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 1 Signal Connection 2023-06-13 Support Share: During the past year, SAgro100 automated steering system has helped many farmers in different countries and areas improve their working efficiency and productivity. Our support team has also solved various technical questions for our users. In this blog, we have summarized the questions frequently asked by our users and provided relevant solutions, hoping to facilitate your use. Questions during Signal Connection Q1: The software status shows that the satellite is 0.  Detailed description:  In open sky view, the status shows SAT: 0, RTK status: NoGPS Possible scene and solutions: ① After startup, the SAT is always 0. Solution: Check whether the antenna port is loose or unplugged. If not, replace the cables or antennas. ② There is a problem in the ignition process. Solution: The ignition voltage drop caused by battery aging is too large, which does not affect the use and will return to normal later. ③ SAT becomes 0 while driving Solution: The battery voltage is unstable, add a voltage regulator module Q2: The software status shows network error.  Detailed description:  Net Error is displayed in the upper right corner of the software. Possible scene and solutions: ① The network is not enabled Solution: Restart the network in Tablet Settings ② Carrier information is not obtained. Solution: Select a new operator in APN and restart ③ 4G card failure Solution: Replace with a new SIM card Q3: The RTK status shows float.  Detailed description:  The satellite searching is normal (more than 20 satellites searched) but the RTK status shows float. Possible scene and solutions: ① External radio mode Solution :  Check the installation configuration of the base station, try to install it at a high place, without anything blocking it, increase the transmit power of the base station, and try again. If it doesn't work, change the transmit frequency to another frequency channel. The tractor should work within the working range of the radio base station. ② 4G mode Solution :  Check the network status. Check the satellite search status of the base station, whether it supports full satellite systems.

title: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 2 Auto-Steering
body: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 2 Auto-Steering 2023-06-20 Support Share: In order to help our users get started easier with SAgro100 automated steering system, in the last blog, we have summarized the questions during its signal connection. Read:  FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 1 Signal Connection Today, let's take a look at the frequently asked questions during auto-steering. Questions during auto-steering Q1: The motor doesn't work after turning on the auto steering mode Detailed description:  The electric motor does not work and cannot drive the steering wheel to turn automatically. Solution: ① Go to the main interface and check the status. If the Motor Firmware shows error instead of version info, it means the motor is faulty and needs to be repaired. ② Go to the sensor type interface and check the median value base on your angle sensor type. 90° angle sensor: median value=60°±10° 120° angle sensor: median value=80°±10° 360° angle sensor: median value=240°±10° Turn the steering wheel to the left and the value should decrease, turn the steering wheel to the right and it should increase. If it is the opposite, you need to adjust the angle sensor inversion mode. ③ Go to the motor setting interface to check the motor control mode. It should be the speed mode. ④ If the motor was running normally before, but suddenly stopped, the configuration file may be lost. Try clearing the cache. Q2: The software guidance icon moves backwards while the tractor is moving forward. Solution:  The two GNSS antennas are installed reversed. You need to connect the left GNSS antenna to port 1 on the back of the T10 tablet and connect the right antenna to port 2. Q3: Steering wheel turns in one direction. Detailed description:  When the autosteer function is turned on, the steering wheel starts to turn in one direction to the limit. Solution: ① Reason 1: You can try to manually drive the tractor forward, if the software guidance icon moves backwards, which means the two GNSS antennas are installed reversed, refer to Q2. ② Reason 2: There is a problem with the angle sensor setting. Refer to Q1-② to check whether the angle sensor is installed correctly. Q4: The auto-steering mode automatically stopped ① The auto-steering mode stopped during startup Solution : The tractor voltage is unstable. Add a voltage regulator module ② The auto-steering mode stopped during driving.  Solution : This is because the electric motor senses resistance and then switches from the automatic mode to manual mode, which means the level of the manual steering override is too sensitive. You can modify the manual steering override to 10. Q5: Tractor cannot go straight to the line when start autosteer, resulting in some curves Solution: ① When auto-steering is started, the forward direction of the vehicle should be parallel to the AB line. ② Vehicles should not be too far away from the AB line, which should be less than 30cm. ③ Check the parameters – sensitivity and rotating speed, modify according to your vehicle speed. Other questions about the SAgro100 system during driving will be released in future blogs.

title: Tech Chat | How to set up SV100 as a portable base?
body: Tech Chat | How to set up SV100 as a portable base? 2023-06-27 Support Share: When using SV100 GNSS receiver as base, it can be set up as both CORS station or portable base. In previous blogs, we’ve mentioned how to configure SV100 as reference stations via PC. Today, let us introduce how to set up SV100 as a portable base in the field for your surveying rovers, tractors or construction machines. Preparation SV100 GNSS receiver and SA100 GNSS antenna. External power. SIM card for GSM mode. Other accessories, including 4G antenna/whip antenna, tripod, 30cm extension bar and related cables. Installation To install the SV100 portable base, please follow the following steps: Install the detachable hook on the bottom of the SV100 and it can be easily attached to the tripod. Install the SA100 antenna on the tripod and connect with SV100 via the GNSS cable. If you are using GSM mode, install the 4G antenna and insert a SIM card in SV100. If you are using internal radio mode, install the whip antenna on SV100. Connect SV100 to the external power via DC cable. Note :  If you need to set the base on a known point, please use a tribrach to center and level the SA100 antenna and measure the height from the ground to the ARP of SA100 antenna. The radio module is optional. Configuration Users often need to configure the portable base on site, so using a PC to configure is not as convenient as using a mobile phone. SingularXYZ team provides two methods to configure the SV100 via smart phone. Method 1: Configured via SingularNav Android software, which will be released soon. Method 2: Configured via SV100 webpage: Step 1 Login the Webpage of the SV100 Connect to the WiFi of SV100 (default WiFi password: 12345678) through a mobile phone, the name of WiFi is the SN of SV100. Open the mobile browser and enter the IP 192.168.10.12 to enter the web page of SV100 and log in. Initial username: admin Initial password: admin Step 2 Set Base and Antenna Parameters Go to Device Configuration >> Working Mode, select  Base mode and Click GET to automatically obtain the base coordinates, then click  Start Base. If you are using a known point for base station, please go to Device Configuration >> Antenna Setting to input the antenna parameters first. Enter the measured height and select the Antenna Type as SA100. Then go to Device Configuration >> Working Mode to input the known base coordinates. Step 3: Set Work Modes 1. Internal GSM mode (standard) Go to Device Configuration >> GSM Config to Startup the 4G first. Then go to Work Management >> Data Transmission, click to Config the NTRIP Server channel, enter the Caster address, Port, Mountpoint name and related settings to transmit the base differential data to the NTRIP caster. Then check the Startup box and click OK. 2. Internal radio mode (optional) Go to Work Management >> Data Transmission, click to Config the Radio channel. Set Mode as Transmit, and set the  TX frequency, protocol and power according to your needs. Then check the Startup box and click OK. Now you can connect your rover devices to your SV100 portable base station and start work.

title: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 3 Auto-Steering
body: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 3 Auto-Steering 2023-07-04 Support Share: During the past year, SAgro100 automated steering system has helped many farmers in different countries and areas improve their working efficiency and productivity. In order to help our users to get started easier, we have summarized its frequently asked questions. Read previous parts: Read: FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 1 Signal Connection FAQ | SAgro100 Auto-Steering System – Part 2 Auto-Steering Questions during auto-steering Q6: The driving track has small curves Detailed description:  The overall effect of the work looks straight, but there are small curves if you look carefully.x Solution: ① Check the vehicle parameters to make sure the parameters are accurate enough. ② Modify these two parameters according to your actual speed. Sensitivity: This value determines the speed at which the tractor returns to the guidance line. Usually keep the default value when the tractor speed is 3~4 km/h. When the speed of the tractor increases, the value can be increased appropriately. Transmission Coefficient: It can control the speed of the motor adjustment, the range is 0-320. Usually use the default. When the steering wheel is turned too slowly, it can be increased, and when the steering wheel is turned too fast, it can be decreased. ③ Check if the motor is installed firmly, make sure it does not shake during driving. ④ Check the installation of the agricultural implement to make sure it is stable and does not shake. Q7: The driving track has big curves Solution: ① Go to the main interface and check the status. If the Motor Firmware shows 0 or error instead of version info, it needs to be reinstalled or replaced. ② Go to the sensor type interface and check the median value base on your angle sensor type. 90° angle sensor: median value=60°±10° 120° angle sensor: median value=80°±10° 360° angle sensor: median value=240°±10° If not, the angle sensor needs to be reinstalled or replaced. Turn the steering wheel to the left and the value should decrease, turn the steering wheel to the right and it should increase. If it is the opposite, you need to adjust the angle sensor inversion mode. ③ If the system has not been used for a long time, it is necessary to re-calibrate the system. ④ The software file is corrupted. You need to record the vehicle parameters and export the data, then delete the software folder in the system folder and restart. Q8: Deviate from the AB line when driving, deviate left and right and the value is not fixed Solution: ① The ground is not flat, and there are large undulations that cause the vehicle body to tilt left and right. The deviation in this case is a normal phenomenon. ② Slow down and reduce the sensitivity parameter. ③ Check the vehicle parameters to make sure the parameters are accurate enough. Q9: Deviate from the AB line when driving, with a fixed deviation in one direction Solution: ① There is a transverse slope in the working plot, turn on the lateral slope compensation. ② Check the median value. Q10: The implement width is standard, but the spacing is wider on one side and narrower on the other. Solution: ① Check whether the implement is wider on one side and narrower on the other. Push the tool towards the wider side. If the implement cannot be pushed, the simulated farm tool can be used to push 1/4 of the difference of the spacing. ② Check whether the vehicle parameter has an offset in one direction. Th

title: Tech Chat | Which RTK Rover is Right for you? – Differences between SingularXYZ Rovers
body: Tech Chat | Which RTK Rover is Right for you? – Differences between SingularXYZ Rovers 2023-07-11 Chloe Share: Since last year, SingularXYZ team has released multiple RTK devices. Some users may be confused about which one to choose. Today we have selected four of the most popular receivers – P1 GNSS receiver, T8 Pro GNSS tablet, Sfaira ONE network rover and Y1 GNSS receiver, to compare the main features of each RTK device and help you find the most suitable working scene. Common Advantage of SingularXYZ RTK Devices No matter for these four RTK receivers, or other RTK solutions like SV100 base station solution, SAgro100 auto-steering solution, etc., the first thing SingularXYZ team guarantees is its satellite tracking and positioning performance.  With full-constellation tracking & advanced anti-interference algorithm, each SingularXYZ RTK device shows powerful GNSS positioning capability even in some harsh environments. Applicable Working Scenarios of Each RTK We use the following table to make a clear comparison of these 4 RTK devices. RTK Rover Device Channels Main Features Suitable Applications P1 GNSS Receiver 184  4G Transmission Data returning Wearable & portable Handheld for GIS tasks. Wearable for Personnel Tracking. Work with range pole for Surveying Measure AB guidance lines & boundary for agricultural systems. T8 Pro GNSS Tablet 1408 All-in-one design of GNSS antenna, GNSS module and PDA. For both handheld and pole-installed. Handheld for GIS tasks. Work with geodetic antenna for Professional Surveying. Measure AB guidance lines & boundary for agricultural systems. Sfaira ONE Network Rover 1408 Ultra-portable size. Simple operation. Bluetooth connection.  For Professional Surveying in areas with good network conditions. Measure AB guidance lines & boundary for agricultural systems. Y1 GNSS Receiver 1598 Full functionality. 15km enhanced internal UHF. 4G/WiFi/Bluetooth For Professional Surveying in any environment. Measure AB guidance lines & boundary for agricultural systems. Choosing the right RTK receiver for your tasks can greatly increase your productivity. SingularXYZ team is now preparing more RTK receiver choices for you, and I believe they will meet you soon.

title: Tech Chat | How to conduct PPK survey tasks? – Part 1
body: Tech Chat | How to conduct PPK survey tasks? – Part 1 2023-07-18 Zemel Share: In daily GNSS surveying, RTK should be the most popular survey method, which can improve the positioning accuracy to centimeter-level based on the real-time correction data sent from the base station. Normally, the correction data was sent at a frequency of 1 Hz. However, when we cannot get a stable datalink between base and rover, or we need a higher positioning frequency like drone surveying, RTK surveying cannot always meet our demands. That's why we need the PPK survey method. What is PPK survey? PPK (Post-Processing Kinematics) measurement is a GNSS positioning technology that uses carrier phase for post-difference, and is a dynamic post-processing measurement technology. In other words, PPK doesn’t need a real-time datalink for differential data transmission. GNSS raw data will be recorded and then post-processed in the office to obtain accurate position information. The technology uses dynamic initialization OTF (On The Flying) to quickly resolve the full circumferential ambiguity and can resolve spatial 3D coordinates at the centimeter level in 10s to 30s of observation during the field survey. Without the transmission limit of the real-time data link, you can get positioning data at a frequency of 5Hz, 10 Hz and even higher, which is perfect for high-speed drone surveying. How to do PPK survey? Preparation: GNSS Receiver × 2 SingularSurv software Android PDA device SingularXYZ PP post-processing software Other accessories, like tripod, centering role, etc. Step 1: Set up the base station and collect static data. 1. Set up the Y1 GNSS receiver at the base point. 2. Long press the record button on the front panel or config in the SingularSurv software to start static data recording. Step 2: Initialize the rover for PPK survey. 1. Connect Y1 GNSS receiver with SingularSurv software. Go to Survey >> PPK >> File to add a new PPK file and open it. 2. After creating a new PPK file, Click the Init button and keep the Y1 GNSS receiver stable to finish the Initialization. Step 3: Start Surveying After initialization, you can start surveying in the PPK survey interface. Step 4: Export data After surveying, download the static data of the base station and the PPK file of the rover. All above are the field operation steps of PPK measurement. In the next blog, we will introduce how to post process PPK data and get accurate positioning data. Tips : PProcessing software can be used for both static data and PPK data post-processing, which supports the standard Rinex format.

title: Tech Chat | How to calculate & divide the plot area based on measured/known points?
body: Tech Chat | How to calculate & divide the plot area based on measured/known points? 2023-07-25 Kim Share: In land surveying projects, we often need to count the area of different plots. When the shape of the plot is irregular, it will take more effort to calculate its area. In today's blog, we will introduce a simple way in SingularPad software of how to calculate the area of a plot and how to divide the plot according to a specific area in the field. How to calculate the area of a plot? To identify a plot, you need to set its boundaries first. Go to SingularPad >> Tools >> Perimeter and Area, add points to set the plot boundary. You can click Button Library to add measured points as boundary or import known points.  Tip: If your point names are in the same format, the software will automatically recognize the numbering sequence to generate the plot. Otherwise, the plot will be generated according to the order in which the points were added. After adding points, you can go to the Preview Map to see if the plot was generated correctly. The perimeter and area will also be calculated and displayed in the software. How to divide a plot by a certain size? After the plot is generated, the plot can be divided into several small blocks according to a certain area. Method 1: Divide by point In this method, the software draws a line from the point you select to divide the plot by the size you enter. Select the point you need in the map, then a text box will appear in the bottom bar, fill in the required area (unit: m2) and click the confirm button, the map will divide the plot according to the area. The coordinates of the divide points will be saved in the point library, you can stake out the point in the Point Stakeout function. Method 2: Divide by line In this method, the software draws a parallel line from the line you select to divide the plot by the size you enter. In addition to the area calculator, SingularPad also has many other useful functions, which will be introduced in future blogs. Tutorials of SingularPad basic functions, SingularPad Software Tutorial Video Series - Watch on YouTube Learn more about SingularXYZ SingularPad software. https://www.singularxyz.com/SingularPad.html

title: Unknown
body: Tech Chat | How to conduct PPK survey tasks? – Part 2 Post-processing 2023-08-01 Zemel Share: In the previous blog, we have introduced how to conduct the field work of PPK survey. In this article, let us learn together about how to deal with the collected raw data and obtain accurate positioning results. Read previous blog:  Tech Chat | How to conduct PPK survey tasks? – Part 1 Field survey Preparation: GNSS raw data of PPK base station GNSS raw data of PPK rover PProcessing post-processing software (contact our support team to download the software: support@singularxyz.com) Step 1: Data Import Open PProcessing software, follow the left navigation bar to do post-processing. Go to Project >> Create to create a new project. You can click Modify to set the coordinate system and geoid model parameters. After creating a new project, go to Observation >> Import files to import the PPK raw data. Select the file attribute as static to import the raw data of base station, and select the file attribute as dynamic to import the raw data of rover. Tips： 1. Please select the corresponding file attributes and format of your imported data. 2. After data is imported, you can right-click the data file to select the antenna type, analyze data or modify related contents. Step 2: Dynamic baseline processing Go to Dynamic baseline on the left. Select the baseline that needs to be processed and click Process. Click OK after the processing is complete. You can see the fixed epoch number and fixed epoch rate. Double click the processed baseline, you can view the positioning data of each epoch. Step3: Data Export After post-processing, you can go to the Map interface to have an overview of all survey points. Go to Dynamic baseline >> Report setup, select the file format you need to export the result data.
